name: Cares Route Trail
name_original: Posada de Valdeón al Mirador del Tombo — circular en el Parque Nacional Picos de Europa
description: Easy 4.3-mile circular hike in Picos de Europa National Park, offering panoramic views from Mirador del Tombo.
region_intro: Hiking around Posada De Valdeón offers access to the Picos de Europa National Park, characterized by its towering limestone peaks, deep valleys, and extensive forests. The region features diverse terrain, from river gorges to high mountain passes, providing varied hiking experiences. The Valdeón Valley itself is known for its well-preserved natural environment and rich biodiversity. Best hiking trails around Posada De Valdeón The most popular hiking route is Vega de Liordes and Senda del Mercadillo Loop, a 12.8 miles (20.6 km) trail that takes 9 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation changes and dramatic mountain scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cares Route Trail, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This trail navigates through the Cares Gorge, offering views above the Cares River. Local hikers also love the Forests of Valdeón Trail, a 6.1 miles (9.7 km) trail leading through ancient forests, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Posada De Valdeón is defined by limestone peaks, deep river gorges, and dense beech and oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy valley walks to challenging alpine expeditions. The routes in Posada De Valdeón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 2,000 reviews. More than 12,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Posada De Valdeón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Posada De Valdeón?
A: There are over 80 hiking trails around Posada De Valdeón, offering a wide range of experiences from easy strolls to demanding alpine expeditions. The komoot community has rated these routes with an average of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Posada De Valdeón?
A: The terrain around Posada De Valdeón is incredibly diverse, characterized by towering limestone peaks, deep river gorges, and extensive forests of beech, oak, ash, and maple. You'll encounter varied landscapes, from the dramatic cliffs of the Cares Gorge to lush valleys and high mountain passes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Posada De Valdeón?
A: Yes, there are several options suitable for families. While many routes offer significant challenges, you can find easier paths, particularly in the valleys. For example, parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209292824/cares-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cares Route Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer a relatively easy experience through the gorge.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Posada De Valdeón?
A: The Picos de Europa National Park, where Posada de Valdeón is located, generally welcomes dogs, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and livestock. Always check specific trail regulations, especially in more sensitive areas, to ensure a pleasant and responsible hike with your pet.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6051790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cares Gorge (Garganta Divina)&lt;/a&gt;, known for its breathtaking views. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/280873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cable Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas, and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1217129010/forests-of-valdeon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forests of Valdeón&lt;/a&gt;, home to diverse wildlife.

Q: Are there any circular walks available around Posada De Valdeón?
A: Yes, circular walks are a great way to experience the diverse landscapes. A challenging but rewarding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1215064679/vega-de-liordes-and-senda-del-mercadillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Liordes and Senda del Mercadillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers dramatic mountain scenery and significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Posada De Valdeón?
A: The best time for hiking in Posada De Valdeón is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the trails are more accessible. Summer offers warmer temperatures, while spring and autumn provide lush greenery and vibrant fall colors, respectively.

Q: Where can I find parking for trailheads in Posada De Valdeón?
A: Posada De Valdeón serves as a key gateway to the Picos de Europa National Park, and parking is generally available in or near the village. For popular routes like the Cares Gorge, specific parking areas are designated, though they can fill up quickly during peak season. It's advisable to arrive early.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking trails if I want to avoid the main tourist routes?
A: While the Cares Route is very popular, the Picos de Europa National Park offers many trails that are less frequented. Exploring routes that venture deeper into the Valdeón Valley's forests or towards higher mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1221281148/collado-jermoso-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Jermoso&lt;/a&gt; can provide a more solitary experience amidst stunning landscapes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Valdeón Valley and surrounding Picos de Europa are rich in biodiversity. Hikers might spot various bird species, including raptors like golden eagles and griffon vultures. Mammals such as roe deer, red deer, chamois, and even otters, weasels, and badgers inhabit the area. Keep an eye out for signs of wildcats, wolves, or even bears, though these are much rarer sightings.

Q: Are there any short hikes suitable for beginners or those with limited time?
A: Yes, while many routes are long and challenging, there are shorter sections and easier paths available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209292824/cares-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cares Route Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, has an easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) section that offers spectacular views without requiring a full-day commitment.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Posada De Valdeón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 2,000 reviews. Reviewers often praise the dramatic mountain scenery, the well-preserved natural environment, and the diverse range of trails that cater to all abilities, from challenging alpine routes to peaceful forest walks.
tips:
Posada de Valdeón smells of mountains, Picos de Europa and lush vegetation. Located in the heart of the Valdeón Valley, it is an area dominated by extensive meadows and beech and oak forests, granaries and native fauna.

A town with incredible natural charm that makes us want to enjoy the mountains more than anywhere else!

The viewing point was opened on 10 August 1964 and features an iron cross and a stone column with a sculpture of a chamois. There is also an engraving listing all the peaks that can be seen from the site, making it easier to identify them.

Cordiñanes de Valdeón is a small mountain village located in the Valdeón Valley, within the Picos de Europa National Park, in the province of León. Although less well-known than Caín or Posada de Valdeón, it offers access to spectacular trails and high-mountain landscapes.
Although bathing is not allowed in this reservoir, the views are spectacular. Surrounded by the beautiful mountains of the Picos de Europa and with a recreational area to enjoy them in a quieter way.

faqs.question: How long does this circular hike typically take?
faqs.answer: This easy circular hike is approximately 6.89 kilometers long and generally takes about 1 hour and 56 minutes to complete, making it a perfect option for a shorter outing.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is rated as an "easy hike" with easily accessible paths, making it suitable for nearly all hikers, including beginners and families. The elevation gain is minimal, around 140 meters.

faqs.question: What are the main sights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Tombo Viewpoint, which offers stunning panoramic views of the Valdeón Valley, the Central Massif of Picos de Europa, and the village of Cordiñanes de Valdeón. You'll also pass through the charming settlement of Posada de Valdeón and near the Cordiñanes Reservoir.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The paths are generally easily accessible. However, the Mirador del Tombo itself is situated on a rocky formation, and the surface can be uneven and potentially slippery in wet conditions. Sturdy shoes are recommended.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Mirador del Tombo hike?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Posada de Valdeón. Parking is typically available within the village, which serves as a common starting point for many routes in the area.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Picos de Europa National Park has regulations, dogs are generally permitted on trails if kept on a leash. It's always best to check local signage or park guidelines for any specific restrictions that might apply to this particular route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Picos de Europa National Park is beautiful year-round, but for comfortable hiking conditions, late spring (May-June) and early autumn (September-October) are often ideal. These periods offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, though autumn can bring stunning fall colors.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Picos de Europa National Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or permits required for day hikes like this one within the Picos de Europa National Park. However, it's always good practice to be aware of and respect national park regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Can I expect to see any wildlife during the hike?
faqs.answer: The Mirador del Tombo is recognized as an excellent spot for birdwatching. You might observe griffon vultures, golden eagles, and Egyptian vultures soaring overhead. The area is also home to chamois, and a stone sculpture of one is even featured at the viewpoint.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it covers significant portions of the El Anillo Vindio (84%) and the famous Ruta del Cares (81%). It also intersects with parts of [CIMA LE10] Panderruedas * Caín (66%) and [CIMA LE15] Pandetrave (60%).

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stay near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, Posada de Valdeón is a well-known settlement in the Valdeón Valley and offers various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options for hikers.
